MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
David DeRubertis, Esq. 818-761-2322 for Plaintiff.
Pamela Ng, Esq. GORDON REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP (415) 986-5900 for Defendant.
Court reporter: Anita Alderson, CSR #11843, 213-471-2966, anita.alderson@ccrola.com, Reported.

RULING - A R G U E D. Ms. Ng argues and requests a court order sealing materials. The Court informs the parties the Judge Pro Tem has laid it all out in his report and recommendation. The Court instructs parties to meet and confer and if they cannot agree, can go back to the Discovery Department.

DEFENDANT COREY LYDSTONE, CARGURUS, INC.'s Motion In Support Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Independent Medical Examination.

Defendants' motion to compel plaintiff's independent medical examination is granted as articulated in the judge pro tem's report and recommendation which is adopted.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Prevailing party to submit an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 with the report and recommendation of Judge Pro Tem John-Paul Deol attached.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Anita Alderson, CSR #11843, 213-471-2966, anita.alderson@ccrola.com, Reported. (302/rbu)

Off Calendar Nov-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ERIC A. PANITZ, Esq. of Panitz Law Group APC (Tel: 562-924-7800) for plaintiff;
Attys. MOLLIE M. BURKS, Esq. and PAMELA Y. NG, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415-986-5900) for defendants Cargurus, Inc. and Corey Lydstone.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER LICHTI's Motion For Post-Order Issue Or Evidentiary Sanctions, Or Alternatively, To Compel Further Responses And/Or Production To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents To Defendant Cargurus, Inc., Set One.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge Pro Tem: John-Paul Deol; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Karen Vilicich, CSR No. 7634, Email: karenvilicich@gmail.com, Tel: 213-471-2966 (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff Deposition
Off Calendar Nov-17-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. PAMELA Y. NG, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415-986-5900) for defendants Cargurus, Inc. and Corey Lydstone;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS COREY LYDSTONE, and CARGURUS, INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff Deposition.

Defendants stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for defendants (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion granted. The parties are directed to meet and confer on a mutually agreeable date for the deposition which shall occur no later than December 9, 2022. Responsive, non-privileged documents requested by the deposition notice shall be produced no later than two business days before the start of the deposition. The Court notes that Plaintiff's primary objection is that Defendants have not complied with their discovery objections. Case law is clear that a party's discovery obligations are independent from the discovery conduct of the opposing party. Stephen Slesinger, Inc. v. Walt Disney Co., 155 Cal. App. 4th 736 (2007). Sanctions of $750 imposed against Plaintiff.

Counsel for defendants is required to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
